audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Sabbath healing, banquet humility, inviting poor/disabled, great banquet parable, cost of discipleship, tower/king images, and salt.

verses.restored: It happened when he went into the house of one of the rulers of the Pharisees on a Sabbath to eat bread, they were watching him closely.
Look, a certain man with swelling was before him.
Jesus answered the lawyers and Pharisees, saying, Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath, or not?
But they were silent. Taking hold of him, he healed him and released him.
He said to them, Which of you, having a child or ox fallen into a well, will not immediately pull it out on a Sabbath day?
They were not able to answer these things.
He spoke a parable to those invited, noticing how they were choosing the first places, saying to them,
When you are invited by someone to a wedding feast, do not recline in the first place, lest someone more honorable than you has been invited by him.
The one who invited you both will come and say to you, Give this one place; then with shame you will begin to take the last place.
But when you are invited, go recline in the last place, so that when the one who invited you comes, he may say, Friend, move up higher; then there will be glory for you before all reclining with you.
For everyone exalting oneself will be humbled, and the one humbling oneself will be exalted.

He also said to the one who had invited him, When you make a lunch or dinner, do not call your friends, brothers, relatives, or rich neighbors, lest they also invite you back and repayment come to you.
But when you make a banquet, call poor people, disabled people, lame people, blind people.
You will be blessed/flourishing, because they do not have means to repay you; for you will be repaid in the resurrection of the righteous.
One of those reclining with him heard these things and said to him, Blessed/flourishing is whoever eats bread in the reign of the Divine.
He said to him, A certain person made a great dinner and invited many.
At the hour of the dinner he sent his servant to say to those invited, Come, because now it is ready.
They all alike began to excuse themselves. The first said, I bought a field, and I have necessity to go out and see it; I ask you, have me excused.
Another said, I bought five yoke of oxen, and I am going to test them; I ask you, have me excused.
Another said, I married a wife, and because of this I am not able to come.

The servant came and reported these things to his lord. Then the house-master, angered, said to his servant, Go out quickly into the streets and lanes of the city, and bring in here the poor, disabled, blind, and lame.
The servant said, Lord, what you commanded has happened, and still there is place.
The lord said to the servant, Go out into the roads and hedges and compel them to come in, so that my house may be filled.
For I say to you that none of those people who were invited will taste my dinner.
Great crowds were traveling with him, and turning, he said to them,
If anyone comes to me and does not hate father, mother, wife, children, brothers, sisters, and even one's own life/soul, that one is not able to be my disciple.
Whoever does not carry one's own cross and come after me is not able to be my disciple.
For which of you, wanting to build a tower, does not first sit down and calculate the cost, whether one has enough for completion?
Otherwise, after laying a foundation and not being strong enough to finish, all seeing may begin to mock him,
saying, This person began to build and was not strong enough to finish.
Or what king, going to engage another king in battle, will not first sit down and deliberate whether he is able with ten thousand to meet the one coming against him with twenty thousand?
If not, while the other is still far away, he sends a delegation and asks the things for peace.
So therefore, every one of you who does not renounce all one's possessions is not able to be my disciple.
Salt is beautiful/good; but if even salt becomes tasteless, with what will it be seasoned?
It is useful neither for soil nor for manure pile; they throw it outside. Whoever has ears to hear, let that one hear.
